summary.intent: Fix a Rust test function signature formatting issue that was causing the format check CI step to fail.
summary.outcome: Reformatted the long return type signature in pool.rs to match nightly rustfmt expectations, committed and pushed the fix.

repo: Workspace lint config denies expect() even in tests — tests must use Result<...> with ? propagation instead.
The project uses nightly rustfmt, which breaks long function signatures onto a new line before the opening brace.
CI runs parallel local checks: zizmor, biome, i18n, and fmt — all must pass.

code.finding: Test fn set_container_dir_permissions_makes_world_writable has a long return type that nightly rustfmt formats by placing the opening brace on a new line.

workflow: Format check failures from nightly rustfmt can produce cosmetic-only diffs (brace placement) that still block CI.
zizmor is used for GitHub Actions security auditing and runs on .github/actions and .github/workflows.

friction: Nightly rustfmt formatting rules differ from stable and silently reformat code, causing unexpected CI failures unrelated to logic changes.
The format check failure was a side effect of a prior fix (replacing expect() with ?), not a direct mistake.
